Prompt for password when importing a keystore (#101)

The import flow was missing a password prompt, so the password was
never stored in secure storage. This caused sync/upload and signature
viewing to fail with 'no saved password'.

Now the import flow:
1. Picks the keystore file
2. Prompts for the keystore password
3. Validates the password by attempting to read the keystore
4. Stores the password in secure storage on success

Fixes #100

commit.message:
Fix serena formula: use sdists + rust build dep instead of wheels

virtualenv_install_with_resources unpacks .whl files (treating them as
zips) then runs pip on the extracted dir with --no-binary=:all:, so
pre-built wheels are not compatible with that helper.

Correct approach: keep all packages as sdists and declare Rust as a
build-time dependency so maturin can compile jiter, pydantic-core,
rpds-py, and tiktoken from source.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initialize documents, security improvements; (#40)

* wip: security improvements

* improve docs & security

* Remove X-Otela-Client-Wallet from CORS allowed headers (#45)

* Initial plan

* Remove X-Otela-Client-Wallet from CORS allowed headers

X-Otela-Client-Wallet is used for access control decisions on worker
nodes and must only be injected by trusted head nodes. Including it in
Access-Control-Allow-Headers allowed browser clients to freely set
the header in cross-origin requests, enabling wallet identity spoofing.

commit.message:
doc(serialization): expand shard serialization design with tiered analysis and roadmap (#6827)

* doc(serialization): expand design doc with tiered analysis and roadmap

Restructure and significantly expand the shard serialization design
document. Rename "Open Questions" (3 sections) to "Inefficiencies and
Improvement Goals" (5 sections) with concrete problem/goal/approach
structure, and add a phased technical roadmap.

Key additions:
- Define self-contained vs baseline-dependent journal entry terminology
- Add "Journal Entries Without OnDbChange" section: table of bypass
  paths (ExpireIfNeeded, Evict, SCAN-based deletion, control signals)
  and analysis of why OnDbChange is infeasible on those paths
- Add counter-example showing harmful byte-stream corruption when
  inline eviction bypasses the ConsumeJournalChange mutex
- Add "Delayed Serialization of tiered entities" section with
  RestoreStreamer note and two notions of bucket completion
- Clarify CVCUponInsert non-usage in non-PIT mode
- Add deferred deletion queue proposal for non-transaction journal
  paths (expiry, eviction)
- Add §4 non-PIT evolution: self-contained journal classification
  with emitted-command-form caveat, and SkipBoth optimization
- Add §5 summary table mapping mutex roles to replacements
- Add comprehensive technical roadmap (Phases 0-4, items 1-17)
- Update flowchart with expiry/eviction path
- Document OnDbChange blocking latency tradeoff for tiered buckets
- Clarify large-value lock-release stability requires both bucket
  versioning and OnDbChange blocking

Fix debug session not terminating when browser closed from elevated VS (#2327)

When VS runs as Administrator, Chrome/Edge is launched unelevated via the
DAP launchUnelevatedRequest. This creates a NonTrackedBrowserProcess with
no PID and a never-firing onExit event. When the user closes the browser,
the CDP connection is lost, but the reconnection retry loop runs forever
because the process exit event never fires to cancel it.

Fix: if launching unelevated, canReconnect is always false

proposal(REQ-SYNC-072): Option G - Transparent UUID Promotion

Add Option G to fix options analysis for issue #8450:

Key insight: Achieve all 4 goals simultaneously:
1. No breakage - Loop overrides continue to sync
2. Special handling - UUID detection triggers promotion
3. No DB poisoning - _id always ObjectId
4. Clean future - identifier field ready from day 1

Implementation approach:
- On POST: UUID _id → identifier field + server ObjectId
- Dedup: Lookup by identifier first, then _id
- Response: Return both _id and identifier
- Index: Unique sparse index on identifier

Comparison to Option A (current PR #8447):
- Same code complexity
- Option A: Mixed _id formats (permanent)
- Option G: Clean _id (always ObjectId) + identifier

Documents:
- GAP-TREAT-012: Add Option G with full implementation

commit.message:
fix(reconcile): use MultiPartitionKey and sanitize tag values

Two bugs surfaced when running the reconciliation script in production:

1. Tag value validation error: Dagster only allows [a-zA-Z0-9_-.] in tag
   values (max 63 chars).  The 'dagster/reconciled_from_partition' tag was
   set to the raw partition key string (e.g. MITx-15.415x-3T2018-course|edge)
   which contains '|', an invalid character.  Fix: added _tag_safe() helper
   that replaces '|' with '__' and truncates to 63 chars.

2. Partition key type: course_and_source is a DynamicPartitionsDefinition
   whose keys are MultiPartitionKey objects (course_id + source_system
   dimensions).  The script was building plain strings for add_dynamic_partitions
   and AssetMaterialization, which may not round-trip correctly.  Fix:
   - Replaced _build_partition_key() with _make_multi_partition_key() that
     returns a proper MultiPartitionKey({'course_id': ..., 'source_system': ...})
   - Use str(mk) for existence checks against get_dynamic_partitions() results
   - Import MultiPartitionKey from dagster

Also added '# ruff: noqa: INP001' since the script lives in dg_deployments/
which has no __init__.py (consistent with how bin/ scripts suppress this rule).

commit.message:
feat: Stadtsuche mit automatischer Koordinatenübernahme (Nominatim API)

Neue Funktion: Der Benutzer kann im Hauptfenster einen Stadtnamen
eingeben und auf 'Suchen' klicken. Die Koordinaten werden dann
automatisch aus der OpenStreetMap Nominatim API übernommen.

Neue Dateien:
  - Models/GeocodingResult.cs:
    Datenmodell für Nominatim-API-Response (jsonv2); enthält lat/lon
    als Strings und berechnet double-Properties mit InvariantCulture.
    ShortName gibt den ersten Teil des DisplayName zurück.

  - Services/GeocodingService.cs:
    REST-Client für Nominatim API mit korrektem User-Agent-Header,
    Accept-Language: de sowie featuretype=settlement-Filter.
    Ergebnisse werden nach Importance absteigend sortiert.

  - Forms/CitySelectionForm.cs + .Designer.cs:
    Modaler Auswahldialog für mehrere Treffer. Zeigt Treffer in
    einer ListBox; bei Auswahl werden Koordinaten + Typ angezeigt.
    Doppelklick bestätigt sofort. Vollständig im App-Farbschema.

Geänderte Dateien:
  - Forms/MainForm.Designer.cs:
    txtLocationName etwas schmaler (155px statt 200px).
    Neuer Button 'Suchen' (btnCitySearch) rechts daneben.

  - Forms/MainForm.cs:
    GeocodingService als _geocodingService-Feld (inkl. Dispose).
    btnCitySearch_Click: ruft SearchCityAsync auf; bei 0 Treffern
    Hinweis-Dialog; bei 1 Treffer direkte Übernahme; bei mehreren
    Treffern öffnet CitySelectionForm.
    Neue Methode ApplyCityResult(): schreibt Koordinaten in UI +
    Settings und persistiert die Konfiguration.
